# NodePrism

> Open-source infrastructure monitoring platform for servers, containers, and services.

NodePrism is a self-hosted server monitoring platform built with TypeScript. It monitors KVM, OpenVZ, Virtuozzo, Docker, LXC containers, and hosting panels like cPanel/WHM. It uses Prometheus for metrics, Grafana for visualization, Loki for logs, and AlertManager for alerts. It includes ML-based anomaly detection and supports 13 exporter types.

## Use Cases

- Monitor dedicated servers, VPS, and cloud instances
- Monitor KVM/libvirt virtual machines with per-VM metrics
- Monitor OpenVZ and Virtuozzo containers with CPU, memory, and network tracking
- Monitor cPanel/WHM hosting panels (accounts, bandwidth, disk)
- Monitor LiteSpeed web servers and Exim mail servers
- Monitor MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis databases
- Monitor Nginx and Apache web servers
- Detect anomalies in server metrics using ML (K-means clustering)
- Send alert notifications via Slack, Telegram, Email, Discord, Webhooks, PagerDuty
- Track incidents with status workflows and resolution timelines
- Monitor uptime via HTTP/HTTPS, TCP, ICMP, DNS checks
- Generate daily infrastructure reports to Slack/Telegram
- Forecast disk usage trends with linear regression

## Quick Start

One-line install on Ubuntu/Debian:
```
curl -sL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/digin1/NodePrism/main/deploy.sh | sudo bash
```

Add monitored servers:
```
curl -sL http://<nodeprism-ip>:3000/agent-install.sh | sudo bash
```

## Detailed Docs

- [Full AI context](https://digin1.github.io/NodePrism/llms-full.txt): Comprehensive documentation for AI agents
- [Getting Started](https://digin1.github.io/NodePrism/getting-started): Installation guide
- [Architecture](https://digin1.github.io/NodePrism/architecture/overview): System design
- [Agent Scripts](https://digin1.github.io/NodePrism/agents/overview): All 13 agent types
- [Monitoring](https://digin1.github.io/NodePrism/monitoring/overview): Prometheus stack and exporters
- [API Reference](https://digin1.github.io/NodePrism/api/endpoints): REST API
- [Deployment](https://digin1.github.io/NodePrism/deployment/guide): Production setup
